text.title: AI Agent Prompt Handover Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every AI Agent Prompt Handover application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Handover MUST include 4 artefacts: prompt inventory, parameter map, runbook, validation suite. Missing any of the four is rejected.
rule.rationale: Each artefact answers a different inheritor question (what / how to fill / how to run / how to know it works); skipping any creates an immediate blocker for the new team.

rule.statement: Inventory MUST list each prompt with name, purpose, owning agent (Claude / Cursor / Copilot), source path, current version, last-tested-on date. Bare lists are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without the full inventory the inheritor cannot tell which prompts are alive vs deprecated; the dates surface staleness.

rule.statement: Every parameter slot in every prompt MUST be documented (name, type, source, example value). Undocumented slots are rejected.
rule.rationale: Prompts fail silently when slots are mis-filled; the parameter map is the only way to reproduce the working configuration.

rule.statement: Handover MUST include a runnable validation suite (>=3 sample inputs per prompt with expected output checks). Suite-less handovers are rejected.
rule.rationale: The receiving team must be able to verify nothing broke in transit and detect regressions; without the suite, drift is invisible.

rule.statement: Each prompt MUST nominate a named in-house owner post-handover. 'Team' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Unowned prompts decay; the named owner is the renewal anchor.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
